title: JSX files are parsed and scanned as plain TypeScript unless they end in .tsx
reasoning: The module recognizes JavaScript/JSX test filenames via the [tj]sx? test-file regex, but JSX parsing is only enabled for .tsx names. Non-test .jsx files therefore go through ts.ScriptKind.TS and LanguageVariant.Standard, so JSX syntax is tokenized/parsed as non-JSX TypeScript. That can skew L and dupMass for JSX sources even though the filename handling implies JS/JSX inputs are in scope.
recommendation: Treat .jsx as JSX in both scriptKind and scanner variant, for example by replacing isTsx with an isJsxLike helper that matches .tsx and .jsx, and selecting ScriptKind.JSX for .jsx.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The characterization matrix includes .ts sources and a .test.ts exclusion case only; it has no .jsx or .tsx source case that would expose JSX parsing/scanning differences.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a measurement case for a .jsx file containing JSX markup and compare it to the legacy oracle or to the same content under a .tsx filename where applicable.
minimumFixScope: Update extension detection in packages/measure/src/main/measure.ts and add a focused JSX measurement test in packages/measure/src/test/measure.test.ts.
